video:video.video:title: The Wolf-Krugman Exchange: Power, plutocracy and political economy
video:video.video:description: In this final episode of their series for The Economics Show, FT chief economics commentator Martin Wolf and Nobel laureate Paul Krugman consider listeners’ questions and comments ranging from a critique of globalisation, increasing inequality and plutocracy, the global appetite for US federal debt, China’s economic future and much more.

video:video.video:title: The Wolf-Krugman Exchange: America vs the world
video:video.video:description: The US last week released its new National Security Strategy – and it wasn’t good news for Europe. The document – which sets out US foreign policy priorities – blasted Europe for undermining 'political liberty and sovereignty,' de-emphasised the threat from Russia, reframed America’s competition with China and put influence over the western hemisphere at the top of the US agenda. In this episode, FT chief economics commentator Martin Wolf and Nobel laureate Paul Krugman discuss what the impact of this new US strategy may be. How should Europe react to this sharp turn from its strongest historical ally? And is Trump handing China the world on a silver platter?

video:video.video:title: In Fukushima's shadow: Japan's pivot back to nuclear | FT Film
video:video.video:description: In the wake of the nuclear disaster in Fukushima, Japan shut down all of its nuclear power stations. Fifteen years on, the country is reconsidering that decision as the rise of AI has created massive demand for cheap low-carbon power. For a country with a traumatic history with nuclear, that is a difficult and delicate decision
